mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held at an exceptionally low level, discount rates and funding stress ease, so equity valuations expand and the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than baseline when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in CPIAUCSL after a low-rate period signals firmer nominal activity and near-term inflation surprise, which supports cyclical earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal disappears once FEDFUNDS exceeds 1%.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE reflects weakening labor demand and higher recession probability, so equity risk premiums rise and the S&P 500 falls over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the effect is not negative in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which should lift earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is accelerating and HOUST is also rising, the economy is in a synchronized demand expansion that improves cyclicals and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 accelerates year-over-year while HOUST rises above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint expansion condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in most triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and inventory overhang, which lowers cyclical growth expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the signal flips sign in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE marks improving payroll absorption and tighter labor conditions, which supports earnings breadth and lifts the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-unemployment improvement does not generate positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising CPIAUCSL paired with a higher FEDFUNDS rate forces real discount rates higher and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-period observations do not show negative 60-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is stable.
